project.name: my-app
project.description: React + Vite template providing minimal setup with HMR and ESLint configuration.

nodes.name: .gitignore
nodes.summary: Version control exclusion configuration that ignores build artifacts, logs, node_modules, and editor-specific files for the React development environment.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Project documentation describing the React + Vite template setup, available plugin options, React Compiler status, and guidance on expanding ESLint configuration for production applications.

nodes.name: eslint.config.js
nodes.summary: ESLint configuration file that enforces recommended rules for JavaScript/JSX files, with React Hooks and React Refresh plugin integrations for development workflow.

nodes.name: index.html
nodes.summary: HTML entry point that serves the application with a root div mounting point and loads the React application from src/main.jsx as an ES module.

nodes.name: package.json
nodes.summary: Project configuration and dependency manifest defining build/dev/lint scripts, React and Vite as core dependencies, and ESLint/Vite plugins as development tools.

nodes.name: vite.config.js
nodes.summary: Vite build configuration enabling React support via the @vitejs/plugin-react with HMR capabilities for fast development iteration.

nodes.name: package-lock.json
nodes.summary: Dependency lock file that pins exact versions of all npm packages and transitive dependencies for reproducible builds across environments.

nodes.name: hero.png
nodes.summary: Binary image asset used as a visual hero graphic in the React application interface.

nodes.name: react.svg
nodes.summary: React logo SVG with gradient effects for visual branding in the application interface.

nodes.name: vite.svg
nodes.summary: Vite logo SVG with filter effects for visual branding in the application interface.

nodes.name: favicon.svg
nodes.summary: Project favicon SVG that displays in browser tabs and bookmarks.

nodes.name: icons.svg
nodes.summary: Icon sprite sheet containing documentation, social, GitHub, Discord, X, and Bluesky icon symbols.

nodes.name: App.css
nodes.summary: 158 lines of scoped styling for the App React component including layout, animations, and visual theming.

nodes.name: App.jsx
nodes.summary: Main React component (116 lines) implementing the interactive counter demo and community links UI with state management via useState hook.

nodes.name: App
nodes.summary: Root React component function that renders the interactive UI with counter state and community resource links.

nodes.name: index.css
nodes.summary: Global stylesheet (100 lines) with design tokens, CSS resets, and site-wide styling applied to all components.

nodes.name: main.jsx
nodes.summary: React entry point (9 lines) that imports React, creates the root rendering context, mounts the App component, and imports global styles.

nodes.name: .claude/settings.local.json
nodes.summary: Local Claude Code configuration for IDE settings and analysis tools.

layers.name: Entry Point
layers.description: HTML bootstrap and initial application mounting point where browser loads the React application.

layers.name: Application
layers.description: React components, stylesheets, and visual assets that compose the user interface and interactive features.

layers.name: Build Configuration
layers.description: Vite build configuration, package management, and linting tools that power development and production builds.

layers.name: Project Support
layers.description: Project documentation, git configuration, and IDE settings that support development workflow.

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: Start with README.md to understand the project's purpose and vision. This React + Vite template is designed to provide a minimal but complete setup for React development with Hot Module Replacement (HMR) and ESLint rules preconfigured.

tour.title: HTML Bootstrap
tour.description: The index.html file is the entry point for the browser. It defines the root DOM container where React will mount the application and loads the main JavaScript bundle.

tour.title: React Application Entry
tour.description: The main.jsx file is the JavaScript entry point that Vite loads. It imports React, renders the App component into the DOM root, and imports the global stylesheet.

tour.title: Main App Component
tour.description: App.jsx is the root React component implementing the interactive counter demo and community links UI. It demonstrates core React patterns: state management with useState, JSX rendering, and asset imports.
tour.languageLesson: React components use curly braces {} to embed JavaScript expressions. The useState hook manages state, while onClick handlers connect interactions to updates.

tour.title: Styling Strategy
tour.description: Two CSS files establish the project's styling approach: index.css defines global design tokens, while App.css contains scoped styles for the App component.

tour.title: Build & Development Tools
tour.description: The package.json, vite.config.js, and eslint.config.js files configure the entire development and build pipeline. These three files form the foundation of the development experience.
tour.languageLesson: Vite uses plugins to enable JSX and HMR. The React plugin transpiles JSX, and ESLint enforces code quality with configurable rules.
